name: commons
description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape + a live `pool` roster snapshot (~/.pool/pool.json); UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades from context. 0.20.0 (2026-05-30) sharpens @D g47 (PR ship discipline) with a learned operational rule — run `gh pr merge` / `--delete-branch` from the CANONICAL repo dir, NOT a linked worktree's cwd (a worktree holds 'main', so merging from inside it errors `main is already used by worktree`); commons @V 1.8→1.9. The git-guard `git worktree remove --force` false-positive (the `--force` token mis-matched as a force-push) is filed as a separate source-fix handoff (g11/g59), not worked around here. 0.19.0 (2026-05-30) adds @D g77 (credential exists-check before register / login — run `secret list` + check known aliases huggingface.token↔hf.token before claiming a credential missing; the 2026-05-30 HF-token incident), shipped in lockstep with its enforcement — the new `secret-guard` 0.1.0 PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook (s7 — rule + enforcement together). 0.18.0 (2026-05-29) renders the M5 registry pod attribution in `_pods_snapshot()` — each rented pod now shows `<id>[<provider>,<status>] @<project> · "<purpose>"` (empty/absent project → `@⚠unattributed`, purpose omitted when empty), surfacing the cross-project down-guard owner (hexa-lang `pod_registry.hexa` project/purpose fields) so a SessionStart reader sees WHICH project owns each billing pod (anima RTSC near-miss fix). 0.16.0 (2026-05-29) sharpens @D g8 into an explicit deny+redirect: ALL direct runpod/vast provider CLI (any verb — incl. read/lifecycle list/create/destroy) + raw ssh/scp/curl + REST/GraphQL API + python/node SDK is forbidden, ALL access routes through `hexa cloud` (full pod lifecycle: rent·down·list·exec·run·nohup·copy·preflight); commons @V 1.5→1.6 · ships in lockstep with the cloud-guard 0.3.0 enforcement bump (s7 — rule + enforcement together). 0.15.0 (2026-05-29) prepends a one-line binding-instruction preamble between the live snapshots and the commons.tape body (snapshots stay live background; only the tape body is framed as MUST-FOLLOW hard rules — parity with the prefs-hook framing). 0.13.0 (2026-05-29 · CLOUD 도메인 M10) repoints `_pods_snapshot()` from the cwd-local `./pods.json` to the GLOBAL M5 registry `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` (cwd-independent · cost-bearing · canonical "what pods are alive + billing right now" SSOT populated by `cloud rent`/`rm`) — renders `<id>[<provider>,<status>]` + `$<cost>` when cumulative spend > 0 so a forgotten billing pod is always visible; AND adds `_providers_snapshot()` reading the M8 catalog `~/.hx/cloud/providers.json` (one-liner `NN providers · cloud providers list|fit|recommend`). Both global ~/.hx/cloud/ → cwd-independent, always visible; absent → silent (no clutter). 0.12.0 (2026-05-28) added `_pods_snapshot()` reading cwd `./pods.json` (superseded by M5 global registry in 0.13.0); pair feature with the `pods-route` hook (auto-injects `--register` on `hexa cloud nohup`/`fire`). 0.11.0 (2026-05-28) drops the per-host `enabled` field + `[os,on|off]` rendering from the roster snapshot — every roster host is treated as always-on; SessionStart now renders `name[os]` per host, pi5-akida exclusion is handled downstream by pool-route's name-substring akida match. Companion ships: pool 0.8.9 + pool-route 0.12.0 (same always-on roster theme).
